transcript.text: Yeah, it's going pretty good right now.
feel like it tends to be pretty up and down, but hopefully it can stay up.
Well, this could be pretty quick.
Just wanted to see with the sales prospects here, anything outstanding right now or feeling pretty good about everything?
I think there's just one that I think you just added an answer to, right?
Yeah, but pretty good on that side so far.
Something that would be really nice to do right now would be to go over the next week's calendar with the sales.
I just want to see what are the sales calls for next week's

We can see if he's needed, so he's going to have a deep dive with their bridge, he's definitely going to take that, and he definitely wants to take the call with the next last on Wednesday, then you said you wanted him in Blue Moves, right?
Yeah, I chatted with him about that one, but I think that would be good for him to join.
And we also have CRB on Wednesday.
I'm good to take that one.
I think you would just have to, like, confirm with him because he rescheduled that.
He probably wants to be on that one because he rescheduled it when he couldn't do it last week.
Yeah, I think it's because he was an introvert.
I think that's how you say your name.
But I'll double check with George anyway.
Next one, you said yes to Grelteo.
Oh, what about this one on Thursday?
That is called Red AI, Deeper Dad.
He took the first call without me.
So I can do this one solo if he's OK with it.
But I wasn't on the first call.
And BitSite is the name of this one.
You could tell Marcel that I can take that one for him.
Venki's at partner at Menlo Ventures.
I think, well, I don't know.
I was thinking that we should be in this channel called Marcel Support.
Just because we talk about things related with Marcel Condor there, with George and Bridget, too.
Oh, can you add me to that?
Bridget said that Marcel should be on that one.
I just, that's my one request.
Like, I think, yeah, I mean, I tried to tell Marcel this yesterday, but any intro call, like, he doesn't need to be on it.
Especially if he's trying to limit the amount of meetings that he's taking.
So I don't know if that has sunk in yet, because he still joins so many of them.

But, like, even the ones that are introduced by investors, like, I can just bring him to the second call, you know, versus, like, meeting, like, if he's there on the first call, then, like, there's almost, like, this expectation that he joins the next one, you know?
I would just, like, you know, stay in communication with me and George.
So that if he keeps joining these intros, like, I can just tell George, like, he doesn't need to.
So I will double check with CRV and Red AI.
The rest are good to go, right?
Yeah, I mean, yeah, I could also take BitSide if you wanted me to, but, yep, I think those are the only two to double check on.
No, BitSide, I'm saying myself should be filming because Richard said in the 24-7, believe.
Just because it's from there a little.
And then just quickly here, like, Are we doing anything in HubSpot together?
Tyler, I know we talked about that before, but have you done anything with that?

So every time, you know, the phone numbers that I got from other emails that I've done, and that I actually have got a response from, I added them to Hotspot, I created contacts for all of them, and then I'm creating deals for the sales calls that you guys have.
started that like three days ago, I think, or something.
And if like a deal already exists, just make sure that you don't create another one.
It should only, you should only need to check for the ones that get scheduled through emails with Marcel.
And, um, I created, I created one for Brex already, the call yesterday.
Yeah, I definitely check before creating it.
And the last thing was, okay.
So, like, Marcel will sometimes introduce me.
And I guess I've, like, I've missed a couple of those and was kind of late with following up.
God, how do I do this?
So, from Marcel, just say, like, Tyler.
Yeah, like, I'm just seeing if I missed any recently.
That one, yeah, that one was really me.
I guess, like, when Marcel just mentions me, do you want to jump on this and schedule it, or should I?
Yeah, that's what I just wanted to make sure I was on the same page about.
I would like to help you out with that, so I'm happy to do it.
I just want to get your email to a good place, because when I do it from yours, you know, I need to also keep it in a place that I can just look at it.
Yeah, like, from the forest, you know, like, I can see them all in, let's say, a waiting reply.
So I'm trying to clean up your emails to help from yours, you know.
I think if you could maybe just like do a quick scan, doesn't have to be too long, but I'm a little worried that I'm like missing one or two of the intros.
Maybe not though, because I did check that earlier this week.
But I guess if you are going to respond and offer times and schedule it from my calendar, I'll just stop like thinking about those, if that's okay.
I don't want to give you too much though.
So one thing that you can definitely help me out for me to help you with that is when you see an email coming in.
Let's see, I'm trying to, for example, if you get a lot of notifications from things that you actually don't read, know, trying to unsubscribing or.
Say you see something and it's like, this is not helpful.
You can just classify it as, you know, whatever, like others.
If it's a meeting update notification, if you see it, you know, like it doesn't have to be something you do every day.
But if you have like five minutes to check your email and you see it, just classify whatever you can, whenever you can.
If I just like put things in to respond and that's all you need to look at is what's in to respond.
That way, because those are the only ones I think you would need to, like, I definitely don't need you to organize my emails into these labels.
But maybe what I can do is if I see things that I would have you respond and offer times for, I can just move it to respond.
So that that's where you need to go and you don't need to go anywhere else.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review sales pipeline and align on meeting schedules for Marcel and Tyler.](https://fathom.video/share/A9UjMzv5x-eJuzyVXzphpXGVdxEoJkEq?tab=summary&timestamp=17.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Sales pipeline is in good shape with minimal outstanding issues](https://fathom.video/share/A9UjMzv5x-eJuzyVXzphpXGVdxEoJkEq?tab=summary&timestamp=30.0)
  - [Marcel's involvement in intro calls needs optimization to manage his time effectively](https://fathom.video/share/A9UjMzv5x-eJuzyVXzphpXGVdxEoJkEq?tab=summary&timestamp=259.0)
  - [Dignory is managing HubSpot entries for new contacts and deals from email responses](https://fathom.video/share/A9UjMzv5x-eJuzyVXzphpXGVdxEoJkEq?tab=summary&timestamp=368.0)
  - [Tyler and Dignory aligned on email management process for scheduling intro calls](https://fathom.video/share/A9UjMzv5x-eJuzyVXzphpXGVdxEoJkEq?tab=summary&timestamp=624.0)

## Topics

### Sales Pipeline Status

### HubSpot Management
